articles.title: At least 17 killed after drone strikes school in Sudan
articles.description:
Strike in Shukeiri killed teachers and health care workers and is latest incident in three-year war
At least 17 people, most of them schoolgirls, were killed on Wednesday when an explosive-laden drone blamed on Sudan’s paramilitary Rapid Support Forces struck a secondary school and a health care centre.
At least 10 people were wounded in the strike in the village of Shukeiri in the White Nile province, according to Dr Musa al-Majeri, director of Douiem Hospital, the nearest major medical facility to the village.
 Continue reading...
articles.titleJa: スーダンでドローンが学校を攻撃、少なくとも17人死亡
articles.summary: スーダンでドローン攻撃により学校が被弾し、生徒や医療従事者を含む少なくとも17人が死亡、10人が負傷。3年にわたる紛争の最新の出来事。

articles.title: French aid worker among three killed in dronestrike in east DRC, M23 rebels say
articles.description:
Rebel group blames government for attack on residential area of M23-controlled city of Goma
Three people including a French UN aid worker have been killed in a drone attack in Goma, a spokesperson for the M23 rebel group has said.
The attack took place at about 4am on Wednesday in the upmarket residential neighbourhood of Himbi in the city, which has been under M23 occupation since January 2025.
 Continue reading...

articles.title: At least 65 Nigerian soldiers killed in jihadist raids in country’s north-east
articles.description:
Gunmen from Islamic State West Africa Province overran four military bases and abducted 300 civilians, say reports
At least 65 Nigerian soldiers have been killed in jihadist raids across the country’s north-east in the last two weeks, as the west African state battles to contain one of the world’s deadliest terror groups.
On 5 and 6 March, gunmen from Islamic State West Africa Province (Iswap) overran four military bases in Borno state, the epicentre of the insurgency. Nigerian daily the Punch reported that about 40 soldiers were killed in total in these attacks.
 Continue reading...
articles.titleJa: ナイジェリア北東部で聖戦士による襲撃、少なくとも兵士65人が死亡
articles.summary: ナイジェリア北東部でイスラム国西アフリカ州の聖戦士が軍事基地を襲撃し、過去2週間で兵士65人が死亡。300人の民間人が拉致された模様。

articles.title: Experts fear ‘unethical’ vaccine trial in Africa is ‘prototype’ for US studies under RFK Jr
articles.description:
Danish researchers whose work on effects of vaccines has been called into question are at center of US vaccine policy
New details are leading experts to fear that an “unethical” vaccine trial in Guinea-Bissau is the “prototype” for studies under Robert F Kennedy Jr, secretary of the US department of health and human services (HHS) and longtime vaccine critic.
At the center of US vaccine policy is an unlikely set of Danish researchers whose work on the health effects of vaccines has been called into question. The study in Guinea-Bissau would have looked at the overall health effects of giving hepatitis B vaccines by only vaccinating half of the newborns in the study at birth despite an 18% prevalence rate in adults of the illness, which can lead to serious and sometimes fatal health consequences.
 Continue reading...
articles.titleJa: アフリカでの「非倫理的」ワクチン治験が、RFK Jr指揮下の米国研究の「原型」になると専門家が懸念
articles.summary: ギニアビサウにおける「非倫理的」なワクチン治験が、RFK Jr氏が関わる米国での研究の「原型」になる可能性があり、専門家が懸念を表明。

articles.title: Prison sentences for pair who attacked gay men hailed as sign of hope for Kenya’s LGBTQ+ community
articles.description:
The perpetrators were jailed for 15 years for robbery with violence in the east African country, where homophobic attacks are increasing
The sentencing of two people who attacked and robbed two gay men in Kenya has been hailed by LGBTQ+ rights advocates as a breakthrough and a sign of hope for the country’s queer community. “Abel Meli & Another” were sentenced to 15 years in prison for robbery with violence on 3 March at Milimani law courts in Nairobi.
The ruling is a rare example of justice being served for the queer community in Kenya. Njeri Gateru, the executive director of the National Gay and Lesbian Human Rights Commission, an independent human rights institution working towards equality for sexual and gender minorities in Kenya, said: “A lot is going against [the queer community] with the existence of the criminal laws and prevailing homophobic attitudes, but some of us still trust that we can find justice, so this case encourages us.”
 Continue reading...
articles.titleJa: ケニアでゲイ男性を襲撃した二人への判決、LGBTQ+コミュニティに希望の兆しと称賛
articles.summary: ケニアでゲイ男性を襲撃し強盗傷害の罪で15年の懲役刑が下された。これは同国で増加する同性愛嫌悪的攻撃に対し、LGBTQ+コミュニティにとって正義と希望の兆しと歓迎されている。

articles.title: El Salvador’s mass arrest policy may have led to crimes against humanity, study shows
articles.description:
Experts documented murder, torture and disappearances under president Nayib Bukele’s policy targeting gangs
The draconian mass incarceration policy of El Salvador’s president, Nayib Bukele, may have led to crimes against humanity, according to a new study by legal experts.
By locking up 1.4% of the population without due process, Bukele turned El Salvador from one of Latin America’s most violent countries into one of its least violent – but at the cost of human rights and the rule of law.
 Continue reading...
articles.titleJa: エルサルバドルの大量逮捕政策が人道に対する罪に繋がった可能性、調査が示す
articles.summary: エルサルバドルのブケレ大統領によるギャング対策の大量逮捕政策が、殺人、拷問、失踪といった人道に対する罪を引き起こした可能性があると、新たな研究が発表。

articles.title: Argentina grants asylum to Brasília rioter in move that may sway Brazil vote
articles.description:
Decision to shield pro-Bolsonaro truck driver sentenced for 8 January 2023 attack could inflame Brazil election politics
Argentina has granted asylum to a Brazilian fugitive convicted for his role in 2023 pro-Bolsonaro riots – a decision that analysts say could reverberate in Brazil’s upcoming presidential election.
A week after Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva, Brazil’s president, took office, hundreds of people ransacked Brazil’s congress building, presidential palace and supreme court on 8 January 2023, in an attempt to overturn former president Jair Bolsonaro’s electoral defeat. Investigators later concluded the attacks were the culmination of a broader plot aimed at staging a coup.
 Continue reading...
articles.titleJa: アルゼンチン、ブラジリア暴動参加者に亡命を許可 – ブラジル選挙に影響か

articles.title: José Antonio Kast, the Pinochet fan about to swerve Chile to the far right
articles.description:
The new president won office by promising to clean up crime, but his background is red rag to a bull for many
Just south of Santiago, the tiny rural town of Paine is a quiet grid of painted adobe facades, shaded squares and shuttered shop fronts as the summer holidays draw to a close.
But the white-knuckle fear of crime that propelled its most famous son, José Antonio Kast, to a resounding victory in December’s presidential election is as present in sleepy Paine as it is the length of Chile.
 Continue reading...
articles.titleJa: ピノチェト支持者、ホセ・アントニオ・カスト氏がチリを極右に転向させる

articles.title: Haiti president’s assassination driven by greed and power, US prosecutors say
articles.description:
Opening statements begin in Miami trial of four men accused in the 2021 killing of Jovenel Moïse

Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox

Greed, arrogance and power were the driving forces behind four men charged in the US for the 2021 assassination of Haiti’s last elected president, Jovenel Moïse , prosecutors told a court on Tuesday during opening statements.
Federal prosecutors and defense attorneys began presenting opening statements in the trial in Miami for Arcangel Pretel Ortíz, Antonio Intriago, Walter Veintemilla and James Solages. They are charged with conspiring in south Florida to kidnap or kill Haiti’s former leader. Moïse’s assassination led to unprecedented turmoil in the Caribbean nation, where gang leaders have grown increasingly violent and empowered.
 Continue reading...
articles.titleJa: ハイチ大統領暗殺は貪欲と権力によるもの、米検察が主張
articles.summary: 2021年のハイチ大統領ジョブネル・モイーズ氏暗殺は、貪欲と権力が動機だったと、米国検察がマイアミでの裁判冒頭陳述で主張。

articles.title: Shots fired at US consulate in Canada in what police call ‘national security incident’
articles.description:
No injuries reported but security boosted at US and Israeli diplomatic buildings in Toronto and Ottawa

Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox

Two men fired multiple shots at the US consulate in Toronto early on Tuesday in what police described as a “national security incident”, prompting beefed-up protection for US and Israeli diplomatic buildings in the city.
The individuals approached the consulate in downtown Toronto at about 4.30am ET, exited a white SUV and fired several rounds from a handgun at the consulate, Frank Barredo, Toronto’s police deputy chief, told reporters.
 Continue reading...
articles.titleJa: カナダの米国領事館で発砲、警察は「国家安全保障事件」と発表
articles.summary: カナダのトロントにある米国領事館で何者かが発砲し、警察は「国家安全保障事件」と発表。負傷者はないが、関連施設の警備が強化された。

articles.description: German arms maker Rheinmetall posted surging profits and forecast rapid growth for 2026 — but investors do not seem impressed, sending shares lower.

articles.title: Sir Don McCullin: 'My life, thanks to photography, has had purpose and meaning'

articles.title: These are the 50 best cities in the world right now – and 19 are in Europe

articles.title: The hidden battlefield: Censorship in the Israel–Iran war
articles.description: Missiles fly between Israel and Iran, but censorship and access limits mean much of the war remains hidden from view.

articles.title: UK court rejects bid to reinstate ‘terrorism’ charge against Kneecap rapper

articles.title: FIFA World Cup: US war on Iran, Mexico violence, visa bans, Iraq qualifier

articles.title: LIVE: Real Madrid vs Manchester City – Champions League last 16

articles.title: Chile’s new president has praised Pinochet, a dictator. What does it mean?

articles.title: Bangladesh demolish Pakistan who suffer record low score in contest

articles.title: Burundi tea plantations hamper chimpanzee protection

articles.title: Zelensky sends drone teams to Middle East, touting Ukraine's expertise

The EIF’s work is dedicated to harnessing trade and investment for prosperity and sustainable economic growth in least-developed countries (LDCs).
articles.titleJa: 第14回閣僚会議のハイレベルイベントでEIF活動の第3段階が開始

articles.title: Uzbekistan commits to maintaining "strong momentum" to accede to WTO in 2026
articles.description: At a meeting of the Working Party on the Accession of Uzbekistan on 9 March, the head of Uzbekistan's delegation, Deputy Prime Minister Jamshid Khodjaev, said his country is committed to bringing Uzbekistan’s accession process to a successful conclusion this year. Thanking members for supporting Uzbekistan's accession during a very busy period, he reaffirmed the country's commitment to engage constructively with all Working Party members. Uzbekistan stands ready to address any remaining issues in a spirit of partnership and mutual understanding, he added.
articles.titleJa: ウズベキスタン、2026年のWTO加盟に向けて「強い勢い」を維持することを約束

articles.title: From field to factory, football and fashion: MC14 high-level event to spotlight cotton
articles.description: A high-level event on cotton will be held on the eve of the 14th Ministerial Conference in Yaoundé, Cameroon, on 25 March to highlight cotton’s importance to global trade and investment opportunities, particularly for Central and West African countries looking to move up the value chain. The event will feature the launch of the Partenariat pour le Coton Investment Platform, spotlighting of fashion and textile/garment entrepreneurs, and unveiling by FIFA representatives and Cameroon football legends of FIFA's tee-shirts and polo shirts to be distributed under the FIFA Football for Schools programme.

articles.title: The value of the WTO: An economics viewpoint in six instalments
articles.description: Why do economies negotiate trade agreements? If unilateral free trade can benefit an economy, why do governments devote so much effort to negotiating reciprocal tariff reductions and detailed trade rules with their partners? The answer lies in a fundamental feature of trade policy: when governments act independently, their policies affect not only their own economies but also the economies of others.
articles.titleJa: WTOの価値：経済学的視点からの6つの連載

articles.title: Coordinators of Plastics Dialogue ready ministerial statement and other outcomes for MC14

The statement was first introduced by the co coordinators in November 2025 and has been continuously updated to reflect members’ perspectives following intensive consultations.
articles.titleJa: プラスチック対話の調整役が第14回閣僚会議に向けた閣僚声明などを準備

She acknowledged the progress they have made in addressing LDCs’ trade priorities and encouraged LDCs to continue pursuing their trade interests.

Most WTO members welcomed the draft as a basis for consensus at a meeting of the negotiating body on 6 March, although a couple of members said they do not find it acceptable in its current form.
articles.titleJa: 農業交渉議長が改訂草案を回覧、第14回閣僚会議の概要が明らかに

The contribution aims to ensure broad and inclusive participation in the WTO’s highest decision making body.

articles.description: To mark the upcoming International Women's Day on 8 March, the Women Exporters in the Digital Economy (WEIDE) Fund on 6 March announced its plans to disburse US$ 1.76 million in grants in the first quarter of 2026 to benefit 219 women-led enterprises in the Dominican Republic, Mongolia and Nigeria. The first disbursement sets in motion the innovative design of the WEIDE Fund, which introduces a new model of grant financing supported by technical assistance and exposure to a multistakeholder ecosystem for enterprises.
articles.titleJa: 国際女性デーを前に、WEIDE基金が女性主導企業に176万ドルの助成金発表
articles.summary: 国際女性デーを記念し、WEIDE基金はドミニカ共和国、モンゴル、ナイジェリアの女性主導企業219社に総額176万ドルの助成金を支給すると発表した。
